name: waterfall
title: Waterfall CLI — waterfall-spec workflows

globalOptions.description: Override waterfall-spec repository root (absolute or relative to cwd).
Print planned agent invocation only (default for lifecycle prompt commands unless overridden).
Invoke the real backend (cline / agent|cursor / claude); implies not dry-run.
CLI tracing verbosity for agent planning / invoke.
Same as --log-level silent (waterfall tracing only; agent subprocess still inherits the console unless agent_silent).
Hide agent backend stdout/stderr (stdio ignore). Overrides .waterfall agent_silent= and WATERFALL_AGENT_SILENT.
Force agent subprocess output on the terminal (default when agent_silent is unset).
embed: inline workflow markdown in the agent message; reference: filesystem paths only.

notes: Waterfall is currently in prerelease phase.
~/.waterfall is created on first run when missing (commented examples).

dotWaterfall.description: Optional key=value config files merged before flags and env (cwd file wins over home per key).

syntax.format: One KEY=value per line; leading/trailing whitespace trimmed; # starts a comment line.
syntax.quotedValues: Values may be wrapped in double or single quotes.

files.role: User-wide defaults; created with a commented template on first CLI run when missing.
Working-directory overrides; wins over ~/.waterfall for each key.

dotWaterfall.merge: Read ~/.waterfall then merge ./.waterfall (overlay replaces per field).

knownKeys.description: true: plan agent runs only; false: allow execute when combined with execute_agent.
true: spawn real backend CLIs; false: dry-run only.
How workflow markdown is passed to the agent.
Directory under the spec root for project prompt overrides (default prompts). Set none or - to disable. Use waterfall prompts materialize to snapshot merged prompts here.
Directory for implementation repos / sys link targets. Relative paths resolve from cwd. If omitted, waterfall sys link uses <specRoot>/projects.

Default for waterfall implement when --mode and WATERFALL_IMPLEMENT_MODE are unset: inplace (no git in impl repo), branch-merge-develop (feature branch then merge to develop), branch-with-pr (push feature branch and open PR via gh when available).
true: agent backend subprocess does not stream stdout/stderr to the terminal (stdio ignore). false/omit: agent output goes to the waterfall console (default). Separate from level= (CLI tracing only).
Optional pack directory (mirrors CLI prompts/: commands/, global/). `.waterfall` only — no env/CLI flag.
Git URL; clone/update under ~/.cache/waterfall/prompt-packs/. Local prompt_pack= wins. `.waterfall` only.
Branch or tag for prompt_pack_git (default main).
Subdirectory inside the pack root before commands/ and global/ (for monorepo layouts).
true: `waterfall export pdf` invokes system `pandoc` (see WATERFALL_PANDOC_ARGS); false: bundled pandoc-wasm + puppeteer.

Tracker adapter id: jira (Jira Cloud REST; stories only — bugs still use direct or future work), or direct (remote snapshot from CLI hint JSON, same shape as sync --dump-spec; sync always applies remote comments to spec then diffs; direct also appends source=waterfall-direct-out JSON after the report). Each kind has its own provider. Jira options: sync_story_jira_site or sync_story_jira_cloud_site, sync_story_jira_project or sync_story_jira_cloud_project, sync_story_jira_cloud_issue_type (default Story), sync_story_jira_cloud_closed_statuses (comma-separated status names → closed), sync_story_jira_cloud_open_only, sync_story_jira_cloud_story_label_prefix. Auth only via env: JIRA_CLOUD_EMAIL and JIRA_CLOUD_TOKEN.
Authoritative side when spec and remote disagree for the same id (`waterfall` = spec tree wins, `remote` = tracker wins). Defaults: STORY → waterfall, BUG → remote.

dotWaterfall.keyMatching: Known keys are matched case-insensitively with - and _ normalized to _ for comparison.

environmentPassthrough.rule: Any KEY that looks like UPPER_SNAKE (^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$) and is not consumed as a known key is stored and merged into process.env for agent subprocesses only where env is unset.

environment.description: Environment variables read by waterfall-cli or inherited by agent backend processes.
environment.precedenceNote: For CLI settings mirrored by flags, precedence is: argv > WATERFALL_* env > .waterfall > defaults (see config.precedence).

variables.name: WATERFALL_SPEC_ROOT
variables.description: Override waterfall-spec repository root (absolute or relative to cwd).

variables.name: WATERFALL_DRY_RUN
variables.description: Print planned agent invocation only (default for lifecycle prompt commands unless overridden).

variables.name: WATERFALL_EXECUTE_AGENT
variables.description: Invoke the real backend (cline / agent|cursor / claude); implies not dry-run.

variables.name: WATERFALL_LOG_LEVEL
variables.description: CLI tracing verbosity for agent planning / invoke.

variables.name: WATERFALL_AGENT_SILENT
variables.description: Hide agent backend stdout/stderr (stdio ignore). Overrides .waterfall agent_silent= and WATERFALL_AGENT_SILENT.

variables.name: WATERFALL_MMDC
variables.description: Absolute path to the Mermaid CLI binary (`mmdc`) for `waterfall export pdf`; overrides PATH and npx fallback.

variables.name: WATERFALL_PANDOC
variables.description: Absolute path to the `pandoc` binary when `use_native_pandoc=true`; overrides PATH and built-in Homebrew guesses.

variables.name: WATERFALL_PANDOC_PDF_ENGINE
variables.description: When `use_native_pandoc=true`: LaTeX engine for Pandoc (default `xelatex`, for Unicode in specs). Override e.g. `pdflatex` or `lualatex` if needed.

variables.name: WATERFALL_PANDOC_ARGS
variables.description: When `use_native_pandoc=true` in .waterfall: extra arguments (space-separated) after input markdown, `-o` PDF path, TOC, and `--pdf-engine` (so you can append another `--pdf-engine` to override).

categories.title: Cline backend
categories.description: Read when invoking the cline-ollama backend (--backend cline-ollama).

variables.name: WATERFALL_CLINE_MODEL
variables.description: Optional model id passed to the Cline CLI as -m when set.

categories.title: Agent subprocess environment
categories.description: Cursor, Claude Code, and Cline run with the current process environment. Set provider keys and endpoints per each tool’s docs. UPPER_SNAKE keys from ~/.waterfall / .waterfall are merged into process.env when unset; see dotWaterfall.environmentPassthrough (examples: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CURSOR_API_KEY, OLLAMA_HOST).

categories.title: Testing / internal
categories.description: Used by the waterfall-cli Vitest suite; operators do not need these.

variables.name: WATERFALL_TEST_HOME
variables.description: When set, used as the home directory for resolving ~/.waterfall.

variables.name: VITEST
variables.description: When "true", skips auto-creating ~/.waterfall during tests and adjusts git helpers.

operatorHint.summary: Operator hints (steering text for agents, or descriptions for scaffolds) are resolved from argv after fixed command arguments.

modes.description: Default: all remaining argv tokens are joined with single spaces (shell quoting controls word boundaries).
Exactly one token "-" reads the entire hint from standard input as UTF-8.
Exactly one token whose value starts with @ reads the file path after @ (relative paths resolve against cwd).

specResolution: walk up to directory containing CURSOR.md

commandGroups.title: CLI & workspace
commandGroups.description: Version, help, scaffolding a new spec tree, queries (dependencies, list), release tags.

commandGroups.title: Implement (linked code)
commandGroups.description: Run the agent in implementation repos linked from SYS (pom.json sysLinks).

commandGroups.title: Remote sync
commandGroups.description: Compare and merge work items with external trackers (see .waterfall sync_*).

commandGroups.title: Export & prompt tooling
commandGroups.description: PDF export and merged prompt pack materialization.

commandGroups.title: Items & change request
commandGroups.description: Create CR, SYS, BUG, HOR; link repos; finish the change request branch.

commandGroups.title: Lifecycle (single step)
commandGroups.description: One prompt per edge: CR→RQ, RQ→UC, UC→STORY, UC→TEST, TEST→STORY, comments.

commandGroups.title: Lifecycle (orchestration)
commandGroups.description: Chained agent steps (cr all, rq all, uc all, test all).

commandGroups.title: Close items
commandGroups.description: Close STORY and BUG folders on develop.

commands.summary: Print installed CLI version.
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

commands.summary: Print usage text or machine-readable CLI spec as JSON.
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

commandOptions.description: Output format; use "json" for the CLI command/options spec.

commands.summary: Create a new spec directory: pom.json (project title + future metadata), layout, CURSOR.md, git on develop.
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root resolution; initializes DIR (may create git there on develop). After that, follow CURSOR.md branch rules in the new tree.

commandOptions.description: Required. Project name written to pom.json at the init root (also used as export pdf document title when that tree is the spec).

positionals.name: DIR
positionals.description: Target directory (default: .).

commands.summary: Print upstream and downstream typed ids for one work item as JSON (identity **CR:**/**UC:**/… fields, ### Downstream references, and reverse links from other items).
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

positionals.name: item-type
positionals.description: Artifact family: cr, rq, uc, story, tst, sys, bug, hor (aliases: st, test, horizontal).

positionals.name: item-id
positionals.description: PREFIX-NNN (e.g. STORY-022) or numeric NNN; must match item-type.

commands.summary: Print one canonical id per line (sorted) for work items of the given type. By default excludes closed items (**Status:** closed, or _STORY/_BUG folders). Optional `in SYS-001` filters by path segment or identity parent fields in the item markdown.
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

commandOptions.description: Include items whose **Status:** is closed (and closed story/bug folders).

positionals.name: item-type
positionals.description: Artifact family: cr, rq, uc, story, tst, sys, bug, hor (aliases: st, test, horizontal).

positionals.name: in
positionals.description: Literal word in, then ancestor id (e.g. in SYS-001).

positionals.name: ancestor-id
positionals.description: PREFIX-NNN — restrict to items under this ancestor in the tree or mentioning it in identity fields.

commands.summary: Resolve SYS from the story path, require pom.json sysLinks for that SYS, then in the linked implementation repo: by default checkout develop and branch feature/<id> (unless --mode inplace). After a successful agent run: merge feature→develop (branch-merge-develop), or push + gh pr create (branch-with-pr). Default mode: .waterfall implement_mode= or branch-merge-develop. Runs prompts/commands/implement-story.md with full upstream/downstream ids in the agent system segment.
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

commandOptions.description: Git workflow in the implementation repo. Overrides WATERFALL_IMPLEMENT_MODE and .waterfall implement_mode=.

positionals.name: item-id
positionals.description: STORY-NNN or numeric NNN.

positionals.name: hint
positionals.description: Optional operator notes appended to the agent hint.

commands.summary: Same as implement story for BUG-*: SYS resolution, sysLinks, optional git branch workflow (see --mode and implement_mode=), implement-bug.md and dependency JSON.
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

commandOptions.description: Git workflow in the implementation repo. Overrides WATERFALL_IMPLEMENT_MODE and .waterfall implement_mode=.

positionals.name: item-id
positionals.description: BUG-NNN or numeric NNN.

positionals.name: hint
positionals.description: Optional operator notes appended to the agent hint.

commands.summary: On develop: create a lightweight git tag at HEAD. Tag name must look like v1.2.4 (leading "v" plus three dot-separated non-negative integers; no unnecessary leading zeros). Duplicate tag names fail with a git error.
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must be "develop".

positionals.name: version
positionals.description: Tag to create (e.g. v1.2.4). Format is validated before invoking git.

commands.summary: Load STORY-* from technical/ into canonical work-item JSON (comments from `STORY-NNN.comments.json` + tracker; compared by author `email` + `createdAt` + display `user` + body). Spec-side attachments are **other files in the same folder** as STORY-NNN.md (not the .md itself). Configure via .waterfall `sync_story_*` (`sync_story_leading=waterfall|remote`, default waterfall). On mismatches the leading side is canonical for state/title/comments/attachments for **reporting**. **Comments on disk** are always overwritten from the remote snapshot for each id present in both spec and remote (`{STORY}-NNN.comments.json`). --dump-spec prints spec-side meta only (ignores hint). Optional hint after flags: same resolution as operator hints (inline argv, `-` stdin, `@PATH`); provider `direct` requires JSON in the `--dump-spec` shape for the remote side. With `direct`, stdout ends with pretty-printed JSON (`source`: `waterfall-direct-out`) after every run (pipe to seed the next `@PATH` hint).
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

commandOptions.description: Emit waterfall-spec work-item meta JSON to stdout (no .waterfall sync_story_* required).

positionals.name: hint
positionals.description: Inline argv tokens (joined with spaces), a single - (read stdin UTF-8), or a single @PATH (read file UTF-8; PATH relative to cwd unless absolute).

commands.summary: Same as sync stories for BUG-* (`BUG-NNN.comments.json` + tracker comments; folder file attachments) using sync_bug_* keys (`sync_bug_leading` defaults to remote). With `sync_bug_leading=remote`, remote-only bug ids are created under `technical/SYS-NNN-*/BUG-NNN-slug/` with **Draft steering (remote import)** for the agent; **SYS** comes from remote `sysId` when set, else the only `SYS-*` folder, else keyword overlap vs SYS/STORY folder names (ties → set `sysId` in the hint). After materialize + comments, runs `prompts/commands/sync-bugs-refine-imports.md` (dry-run or `--execute-agent`). Optional hint, `direct` provider, and `waterfall-direct-out` stdout after each run behave like sync stories.
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

commandOptions.description: Emit waterfall-spec BUG work-item meta JSON to stdout (no sync_bug_* required).

positionals.name: hint
positionals.description: Inline argv tokens (joined with spaces), a single - (read stdin UTF-8), or a single @PATH (read file UTF-8; PATH relative to cwd unless absolute).

commands.usage: export pdf [--mode snapshot|release] [--items LIST | --items=LIST] [-o PATH | --output PATH]
commands.summary: Merge selected spec markdown into one document, render fenced Mermaid diagrams to PNG via bundled @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli (mmdc), then produce PDF with pandoc-wasm (Markdown→HTML) and puppeteer (HTML→PDF, Chromium bundled). --mode snapshot (default): any branch; dirty working tree allowed; default PDF <spec-root>/output/<basename>-<short-hash>.pdf. --mode release: only on develop with a clean working tree; default PDF <spec-root>/docs/<basename>-<tag-or-short-hash>.pdf (tag name when HEAD is tagged, else hash). Optional -o overrides the output path for either mode. --items is a comma-separated list of artifact codes (CR,RQ,UC,SYS,STORY,BUG,HOR,TST,CURSOR); default CR,RQ,UC,HOR,TST excludes CURSOR.md, prompts/, doc/, SYS/STORY/BUG bodies, etc. Document title from pom.json (see spec init --title). use_native_pandoc=true in .waterfall uses system pandoc (WATERFALL_PANDOC_ARGS).
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

commandOptions.description: snapshot: default path under output/ with short hash; any branch; dirty OK. release: default path under docs/ with tag or hash; requires develop and clean tree.
Comma-separated codes: CR,RQ,UC,SYS,STORY,BUG,HOR,TST,CURSOR. Default CR,RQ,UC,HOR,TST. Forms: --items LIST, --items=LIST, -items=LIST.
Output PDF path (overrides --mode default; relative to cwd).

commands.cliForm: export pdf [--mode snapshot|release] [--items LIST | --items=LIST] [-o PATH | --output PATH]

commands.summary: Write the merged effective prompt tree (bundled CLI + `.waterfall` prompt_pack / git + spec `project_prompts/`) into the spec repo. Destination: `project_prompts` relative path (default `prompts` under the spec root) unless `project_prompts=none`.
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

commands.summary: Write the same merged tree to an arbitrary folder (e.g. monorepo subpath) so it can be committed as a standalone prompt-pack repository; layout is always `commands/`, `global/`, `items/` at the chosen folder root or nested path.
commands.branchRequirement: No spec-root git branch check (this command does not resolve a waterfall-spec checkout).

positionals.name: folder
positionals.description: Output directory (absolute or relative to cwd). May be nested (e.g. vendor/acme-prompts).

commands.summary: On develop: new feature/CR-* branch, scaffold CR-NNN.md (next id from existing changerequests/) and commit, then agent prompt cr-create.md to synthesize Summary/Proposed change (--execute-agent or dry-run).
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must be "develop".

positionals.name: description
positionals.description: CR title / slug source. Inline argv tokens (joined with spaces), a single - (read stdin UTF-8), or a single @PATH (read file UTF-8; PATH relative to cwd unless absolute).

commands.summary: On a CR branch: scaffold SYS-NNN.md (next id from existing technical/SYS-* folders) and commit, then agent prompt sys-create.md to synthesize Purpose (--execute-agent or dry-run).
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must match feature/CR-<digits>-… (change-request branch; use waterfall cr create from develop).

positionals.name: description
positionals.description: Subsystem title / slug source. Inline argv tokens (joined with spaces), a single - (read stdin UTF-8), or a single @PATH (read file UTF-8; PATH relative to cwd unless absolute).

commands.summary: On a CR branch: clone git URL under projects_dir (or use a named folder; updates sysLinks in pom.json and commit). Default projects root is <specRoot>/projects; override with projects_dir in .waterfall.
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must match feature/CR-<digits>-… (change-request branch; use waterfall cr create from develop).

positionals.name: SYS-NNN
positionals.description: Subsystem id; technical/SYS-NNN-* must exist.

positionals.name: git-url|project-name
positionals.description: Remote git URL (cloned next to basename) or a single-segment folder name under projects_dir (created if missing; need not be a git repo).

commands.example: waterfall bug create SYS-001 "Cart total rounds incorrectly"
commands.summary: On develop: scaffold BUG-NNN.md under the matching SYS folder (next BUG id from existing BUG-* folders in the tree) and commit, then agent prompt bug-create.md to synthesize Summary (--execute-agent or dry-run).
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must be "develop".

positionals.name: sysId
positionals.description: Parent subsystem id (SYS-NNN); must match an existing technical/SYS-NNN-* folder.

positionals.name: hint
positionals.description: Inline argv tokens (joined with spaces), a single - (read stdin UTF-8), or a single @PATH (read file UTF-8; PATH relative to cwd unless absolute).

commands.example: waterfall horizontal create story database-model "Shared schema for orders"
commands.summary: On a CR branch: scaffold HOR-NNN.md + bump next.HOR (commit), then agent prompt horizontal-create.md to synthesize Purpose (--execute-agent or dry-run).
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must match feature/CR-<digits>-… (change-request branch; use waterfall cr create from develop).

positionals.name: anchor
positionals.description: Anchor item type (e.g. story).

positionals.name: name-slug
positionals.description: Horizontal name slug (e.g. database-model).

positionals.name: purpose
positionals.description: Purpose text. Inline argv tokens (joined with spaces), a single - (read stdin UTF-8), or a single @PATH (read file UTF-8; PATH relative to cwd unless absolute).

commands.summary: On a CR branch: run git diff develop...HEAD for scope; agent prompt horizontal-update (does not edit HOR file mechanically).
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must match feature/CR-<digits>-… (change-request branch; use waterfall cr create from develop).

positionals.name: HOR-NNN
positionals.description: Horizontal id (HOR-001 or 001).

positionals.name: hint
positionals.description: Optional operator hint. Inline argv tokens (joined with spaces), a single - (read stdin UTF-8), or a single @PATH (read file UTF-8; PATH relative to cwd unless absolute).

commands.summary: On CR branch: validate diff vs develop, merge into develop, checkout develop.
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must match feature/CR-<digits>-… (change-request branch; use waterfall cr create from develop).

commands.example: waterfall comment add STORY-001 dev@acme.com Reviewer "Align with API review notes"
commands.summary: On develop: append a remark for any spec item with a canonical `{ITEM-ID}.md` (CR,RQ,UC,SYS,STORY,BUG,HOR,TST + NNN). Stored as `{ITEM-ID}.comments.json` beside that markdown; author identity is `email`, display name is `from`. State OPEN + ISO timestamps; RESOLVED|REJECTED by editing the file.
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must be "develop".

positionals.name: item
positionals.description: CR|RQ|UC|SYS|STORY|BUG|HOR|TST-NNN — must match exactly one `{ITEM}.md` under the spec root.

positionals.name: email
positionals.description: Author identifier for sync (same person as display name).

positionals.name: display-name
positionals.description: Short display label stored as `from`.

positionals.name: hint
positionals.description: Remark text (remaining argv joined with spaces).

commands.usage: story close STORY-NNN [STORY-NNN …] | story close all STORY-NNN [STORY-NNN …]
commands.summary: On develop: close one or more story folders by explicit id (rename with _), one commit per story; close all requires listed ids.
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must be "develop".

positionals.name: STORY-NNN…|all STORY-NNN…
positionals.description: One or more story ids (STORY-001 or 001), or `all` followed by at least one id for an explicit batch.

commands.cliForm: story close STORY-NNN [STORY-NNN …] | story close all STORY-NNN [STORY-NNN …]

commands.summary: On develop: close one bug folder under technical/SYS-…/ (rename with _), set Status closed in BUG-NNN.md, commit.
commands.branchRequirement: Spec repo: current git branch must be "develop".

positionals.name: BUG-NNN
positionals.description: Bug id (BUG-001 or 001).
